 {"id":"xf-59","title":"Task: Unit tests for N+1 replacement (xf-43)","description":"## Purpose
Verify the N+1 loop replacement produces identical results and improves performance.

## Test Cases Required

### 1. Isomorphism Tests (CRITICAL)
- `test_batch_lookup_same_as_individual`
  - Run search with N+1 pattern, capture results
  - Run search with batch pattern, capture results
  - Assert results are identical (order, content, scores)

- `test_batch_lookup_preserves_result_order`
  - Semantic search returns IDs in score order
  - Batch lookup must return in same order
  - Verify order matches for various queries

### 2. Edge Case Tests
- `test_batch_lookup_with_empty_results`
  - Semantic search returns no results
  - Batch lookup handles gracefully
  
- `test_batch_lookup_with_missing_docs`
  - Some doc IDs don't exist in Tantivy (deleted?)
  - Batch lookup omits missing, doesn't crash

- `test_batch_lookup_with_type_filter`
  - Filter by doc_type works with batch lookup
  - Only matching types returned

### 3. Performance Tests
- `test_batch_faster_than_loop`
  - Time 100 individual get_by_id calls
  - Time 1 get_by_ids call with 100 IDs
  - Assert batch is significantly faster (>5x)
  - Log timing comparison

### 4. Integration Tests
- `test_hybrid_search_uses_batch_lookup`
  - Run hybrid search
  - Verify results are correct
  - Verify performance is within target

## Implementation Location
`src/search.rs` test module or `tests/batch_lookup.rs`

## Logging Requirements
- Log timing comparison (individual vs batch)
- Log number of IDs processed
- Log any missing document IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Isomorphism verified (identical outputs)
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance improvement measured (>5x faster)
- [ ] All existing search tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:07.79837819-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:07.79837819-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-59","depends_on_id":"xf-43","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-60","title":"Task: Unit tests for cache invalidation (xf-48)","description":"## Purpose
Verify cache invalidation detection works correctly to prevent stale data issues.

## Test Cases Required

### 1. Stale Detection Tests
- `test_stale_after_db_file_modified`
  - Create cache, record mtime
  - Touch/modify database file
  - Assert is_stale() returns true

- `test_stale_after_embedding_added`
  - Create cache with N embeddings
  - Add new embedding to database
  - Assert is_stale() returns true (count changed)

- `test_stale_after_embedding_deleted`
  - Create cache with N embeddings
  - Delete embedding from database
  - Assert is_stale() returns true (count changed)

### 2. Fresh Detection Tests
- `test_fresh_when_no_changes`
  - Create cache
  - Run multiple searches
  - Assert is_stale() returns false each time

- `test_fresh_when_only_reading`
  - Create cache
  - Read from database (SELECT queries)
  - Assert is_stale() returns false (reads don't invalidate)

### 3. Edge Case Tests
- `test_stale_with_missing_db_file`
  - Create cache
  - Delete database file
  - Assert is_stale() handles gracefully (returns true or errors)

- `test_stale_with_corrupted_mtime`
  - Create cache with invalid mtime
  - Assert is_stale() returns true (safe default)

### 4. Logging Tests
- `test_stale_warning_logged`
  - Create stale cache condition
  - Call get_or_init_vector_index
  - Assert warning is logged

## Implementation Location
`src/main.rs` test module or `tests/cache_invalidation.rs`

## Test Fixtures Needed
```rust
fn create_test_cache() -> (Storage, CacheMeta) {
    let storage = create_temp_storage();
    // Add some embeddings
    storage.store_embeddings_batch(&test_embeddings())?;
    
    let meta = CacheMeta {
        db_mtime: fs::metadata(storage.path())?.modified()?,
        embedding_count: storage.get_embedding_count()?,
        schema_version: 2,
    };
    
    (storage, meta)
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log when stale detection triggers
- Log reason for staleness (mtime, count, etc.)
- Log cache metadata values for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All stale conditions correctly detected
- [ ] No false positives (fresh cache not marked stale)
- [ ] Edge cases handled without panics
- [ ] Warning logged when stale cache used
- [ ] Tests are deterministic (no timing-based flakiness)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:22.284261425-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:17:22.284261425-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-60","depends_on_id":"xf-48","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-61","title":"Bug: Embedding identity collisions (doc_id-only PK)","description":"## Problem
The `embeddings` table uses `doc_id` as the sole primary key. Tweets and likes frequently share the same `doc_id` (tweet ID), which means embeddings for one type overwrite the other. This is a correctness bug: semantic/hybrid search may return the wrong text or incorrect similarity for the requested type.

### Evidence
- Tweets and likes share IDs by design (like references the original tweet ID).
- Current schema: `PRIMARY KEY (doc_id)` in `embeddings`.
- Retrieval APIs (`get_embedding`, `get_embedding_hash`, `get_embedding_by_hash`, `load_all_embeddings`) do not accept/return type-qualified identities.

### Impact
- Incorrect embeddings used for semantic/hybrid results.
- Content-hash de-duplication can reuse embeddings across types incorrectly.
- Potentially wrong ranking and mismatched metadata at retrieval time.

## Goal
Ensure embeddings are keyed by `(doc_id, doc_type)` so that semantic search results are correct and deterministic for mixed-type archives.

## Proposed Fix (High-Level)
1. Bump schema version and rebuild `embeddings` table with a composite primary key.
2. Update all embedding APIs to include `doc_type` in lookups and inserts.
3. Update generation code to pass `doc_type` everywhere.
4. Add regression tests that create collisions (tweet + like same ID) and verify correct embeddings and results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Embeddings table primary key is `(doc_id, doc_type)`.
- All embedding APIs are type-aware; no ambiguous lookups remain.
- Regression test proves tweet/like with same ID return correct embeddings.
- `xf index` still rebuilds cleanly; semantic/hybrid outputs remain isomorphic within type.

## Notes
- Embeddings are derived data; dropping/rebuilding is acceptable with a schema version bump.
- Coordinate with the persistent vector index work to ensure doc_type is stored and used consistently.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:43:23.214129644-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:43:23.214129644-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-61","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-61","depends_on_id":"xf-62","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-61","depends_on_id":"xf-63","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-61","depends_on_id":"xf-64","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-62","title":"Embeddings schema v3: composite PK (doc_id, doc_type)","description":"## Background
Embeddings currently use `doc_id` as a primary key, which collides between tweet and like documents that share the same ID. We need a schema upgrade to make embeddings type-aware.

## Scope
- Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` and rebuild `embeddings` table with composite key.
- Define migration strategy: since embeddings are derived, drop/recreate is acceptable.

## Proposed Schema
```
CREATE TABLE embeddings (
  doc_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  doc_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
  content_hash BLOB,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (doc_id, doc_type)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_embeddings_type ON embeddings(doc_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_embeddings_hash ON embeddings(content_hash);
```

## Notes
- Keep migration idempotent and safe for existing installs.
- Document that re-index is required to repopulate embeddings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema version is bumped and migrations rebuild embeddings table.
- New composite PK exists in the DB schema.
- Running `xf index` repopulates embeddings without errors.

+{"id":"xf-63","title":"Embeddings APIs: make all lookups type-aware","description":"## Background
After switching to composite keys, all embeddings APIs must include `doc_type` to avoid ambiguous lookups and ensure correct semantic search results.

## Scope
- Update `Storage` APIs:
  - `store_embedding`, `store_embeddings_batch`
  - `get_embedding`, `get_embedding_hash`
  - `get_embedding_by_hash` (returns `(doc_id, doc_type, embedding)` or ensures doc_type argument)
  - `load_all_embeddings`, `load_embeddings_by_type`
  - any helper that checks for embedding existence.
- Update call sites in:
  - indexing (`generate_embeddings`)
  - search (`cmd_search`, vector index load)
  - tests.

## Design Notes
- Decide whether `content_hash` is globally unique or should be type-scoped.
- Prefer explicit doc_type parameters rather than inferring from results.
- Ensure behavior remains deterministic (identical results within a type).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All embeddings queries include `doc_type` where needed.
- No ambiguous lookups remain (grep check for old signatures).
- `cargo test` passes and collision test succeeds.

+{"id":"xf-64","title":"Embeddings collision regression test + reindex guidance","description":"## Scope
- Add a focused test that indexes a tweet and like with the same `doc_id`, then verifies semantic/hybrid retrieval uses the correct embedding per type.
- Update docs/help text to clarify that embeddings are rebuilt after schema bump (`xf index --force`).

## Notes
- Use deterministic test data (short texts with distinct embeddings).
- Prefer unit tests in `src/search.rs` or storage tests to avoid heavy fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test fails on old schema and passes after fix.
- User-facing guidance clearly indicates reindex requirement after upgrade.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:03.667856633-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:03.667856633-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-64","depends_on_id":"xf-61","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-64","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-65","title":"Feature: Persistent vector index (mmap) for semantic search","description":"## Problem
Hybrid/semantic searches currently rebuild the vector index by scanning *all* embeddings from SQLite on every invocation. Profiling shows this dominates latency (~70% of samples) and adds ~250ms+ even on modest archives.

## Goal
Persist a compact, type-aware vector index on disk so search can load embeddings via memory mapping (or direct file read) without repeated SQLite scans. This should preserve exact similarity results (isomorphic outputs) while dramatically reducing latency and memory overhead.

## Expected Impact
- Hybrid/semantic p50 latency: target <50ms on ~66k docs.
- Lower per-search allocation and I/O.
- CPU time spent in SQLite row iteration should drop to near-zero.

## Constraints
- Must preserve exact results (no ANN/approximation).
- Must remain privacy-first and offline.
- No format changes that require network access.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Vector index file is generated during `xf index`.
- Search uses mmap/zero-copy load when file exists; falls back to DB otherwise.
- Exact search results and ordering are unchanged (isomorphic).
- Baseline/perf report shows measurable reduction in hybrid/semantic latency.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:43.311780727-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:43.311780727-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-65","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-66","title":"Vector index format spec + validation rules","description":"## Objective
Define a stable, versioned binary format for the on-disk vector index that guarantees exact reconstruction of embeddings and deterministic iteration order.

## Requirements
- Include magic header + version number for forward compatibility.
- Store dimension, record count, and per-record offsets.
- Encode doc_type as small enum or fixed-width string for compactness.
- Store doc_id length + bytes (UTF-8) without loss.
- Store embeddings as raw F16 bytes (identical to DB storage) to preserve exact values.

## Validation
- Header validation with clear error messages.
- Size sanity checks (offset bounds, file length).
- Reject mismatched dimension vs expected embedder.

## Deliverables
- Format spec in `docs/architecture.md` or new short doc.
- Validation routine skeleton (no I/O yet).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec is documented and reviewed.
- Validation logic can detect corrupt or mismatched files deterministically.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:55.835020243-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:44:55.835020243-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-66","depends_on_id":"xf-65","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-66","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-67","title":"Vector index writer: emit file during xf index","description":"## Objective
Write a compact vector index file at index time so search can load embeddings without scanning SQLite.

## Scope
- Generate a vector index file under the index directory (e.g., `xf_index/vector.idx`).
- Prefer a deterministic record order (e.g., by doc_type then doc_id) to ensure stable search ordering.
- Use F16 bytes from DB or the embedding generation step directly (no float drift).

## Considerations
- Avoid extra DB scans if embeddings are already in memory during indexing.
- Ensure file is written atomically (write to temp and rename).
- Include integrity checksum (optional) to detect corruption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Index run produces vector index file with expected size and header.
- File is regenerated on `xf index --force`.
- A missing or partial file does not break indexing (safe fallback).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:08.782312176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:08.782312176-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-67","depends_on_id":"xf-65","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-67","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-67","depends_on_id":"xf-66","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-68","title":"Vector index reader: mmap + exact dot product","description":"## Objective
Implement a read path that loads the vector index via memory mapping and performs exact similarity search without touching SQLite.

## Scope
- Memory-map vector index file (read-only).
- Iterate records and compute dot product using F16 -> F32 conversion identical to current DB load behavior.
- Support doc_type filtering without allocating large intermediate buffers.
- Validate header + bounds before search; fallback to DB if invalid.

## Considerations
- Use frame pointers and debuginfo for perf profiling in development.
- Keep unsafe code forbidden (prefer safe slice views even if slightly slower).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Search path can use mmap index directly.
- Results match DB-based search (isomorphic). 
- Corrupt or version-mismatched files fall back safely with a warning.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:20.822397344-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:20.822397344-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-68","depends_on_id":"xf-65","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-68","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-68","depends_on_id":"xf-66","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-69","title":"Wire vector index into search path + doctor checks","description":"## Objective
Use the new vector index file in `cmd_search` and add health checks so users can detect missing or corrupt vector data.

## Scope
- Search path:
  - Prefer vector index file if present and valid.
  - Fallback to SQLite load if missing/invalid.
- Doctor:
  - Add optional check for vector index file existence, size, and version.
  - Report actionable remediation (reindex).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Search uses vector index automatically when available.
- `xf doctor` surfaces vector index issues clearly.
- Behavior is unchanged when file is absent (current path still works).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:32.189006377-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:45:32.189006377-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-69","depends_on_id":"xf-65","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-69","depends_on_id":"xf-33","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-69","depends_on_id":"xf-67","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"xf-70","title":"Vector index regression tests + perf validation","description":"## Objective
Prove that the vector index produces identical results and improves latency.

## Scope
- Add unit/integration tests that compare vector index results to SQLite-loaded results for the same query.
- Include corruption tests: missing file, truncated file, version mismatch.
- Capture before/after perf numbers using the baseline harness.

## Dependencies
- Depends on benchmark harness (`xf-47`) for reproducible measurements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests demonstrate isomorphism (same IDs, order, scores).
- Perf report shows hybrid/semantic latency improvements vs baseline.

 {"id":"xf-9rf","title":"xf: Full System Investigation, Review, and Performance Optimization","description":"Goal:
- Provide a self‑contained, end‑to‑end plan to understand the xf codebase, audit correctness/reliability, and execute a rigorous performance investigation + optimization program.

Scope:
- Architecture walk‑through (parser/storage/search/CLI/data flow).
- Bug/risk review across critical paths.
- Performance profiling on the provided dataset.

Required Outputs:
- A structured task list with dependencies, estimates, and acceptance criteria.
- Explicit equivalence oracles for correctness (before/after output checks).
- A ranked optimization backlog using (Impact × Confidence) / Effort.
- Minimal diffs: one performance lever per change, no unrelated refactors.
- Rollback guidance for any risky change.

Performance Workflow:
- Establish baseline metrics: p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, peak RSS.
- Profile CPU, allocation, and I/O hot paths.
- Identify and validate bottlenecks with reproducible commands.
- Add regression guardrails (bench scripts, perf test harness, or CI‑safe checks).

Testing & Verification:
- Any fixes include unit/integration tests and an E2E or perf script with detailed logging.
- E2E/perf scripts must log command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and environment.

Constraints:
- Follow AGENTS.md (no file deletion, apply_patch for edits, etc.).
- All changes remain local‑only and preserve output for identical inputs (proof sketch required).

Acceptance:
- Plan is self‑contained and executable without the original instructions.
- Every proposed change includes test + validation strategy.
","notes":"Plan drafted in docs/performance.md (System Investigation & Optimization Plan section); includes tasks, dependencies, oracles, backlog, rollback guidance.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:23:41.616748361-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T14:45:27.431731923-05:00"}
 {"id":"xf-9rf.1","title":"Read AGENTS.md + README and capture constraints","description":"Goal:
- Re-read AGENTS.md and README.md in full and summarize all constraints, workflows, and project goals in a durable form.

Why:
- All subsequent investigation, fixes, and performance work must comply with AGENTS.md instructions (e.g., no file deletion, apply_patch usage).

Steps:
1) Read /data/projects/xf/AGENTS.md carefully (end-to-end). Extract: editing constraints, testing expectations, prohibited actions, perf methodology, and any doc references to best practices.
2) Read /data/projects/xf/README.md carefully. Extract: project purpose, build/run commands, data formats, expected usage.
3) Record a concise summary in a working note (or issue comment) that can be referenced without re-opening the source docs.

Acceptance:
- A clear summary that enumerates all operational constraints and expected workflows.
- Any referenced best-practice guides or doc links are listed for later lookup.
","notes":"Summary (AGENTS.md + README):
- Hard safety rules: never delete files; avoid destructive commands (git reset --hard, rm -rf, git clean -fd) unless user explicitly provides exact command and confirms irreversible consequences. Use safe alternatives first. Document any approved destructive action verbatim.
- Editing discipline: no script-based code changes; modify files manually; no file proliferation (no *_v2.* style files). Use apply_patch for edits; Cargo-only toolchain; Rust 2024 nightly; unsafe forbidden; use explicit dependency versions.
- Testing/quality gates required after substantive changes: cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check; run cargo test and focused tests.
- Project semantics: xf indexes local X archive data; privacy-first (no network in core runtime); parse JS-wrapped JSON window.YTD.*; Tantivy primary search; SQLite FTS5 fallback; preserve metadata (IDs/timestamps/counts) exactly; CLI flags must be truthful; JSON output shape must remain stable.
- README highlights: CLI provides index/search commands; supports types filters, offsets/limits, output formats (json/csv/compact); data archive layout includes data/*.js and assets/; parsing strips JS prefix; storage in SQLite + Tantivy; env vars XF_DB/XF_INDEX override storage; default storage paths by OS; performance targets and benches documented; build/test via cargo; installation via script or cargo nightly.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:24:03.88540847-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:25:28.26120732-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:25:28.261214513-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-9rf.1","depends_on_id":"xf-9rf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-10T02:24:03.909918163-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"xf-9rf.2","title":"Build architecture map + core workflow understanding","description":"Goal:
- Produce a high-level architecture map of xf: major modules, data flow, and runtime workflows.

Context:
- Requires prior understanding of AGENTS.md/README constraints.

Steps:
1) Inventory key modules in /data/projects/xf/src (main.rs, parser.rs, search.rs, storage.rs, index.rs, etc.).
2) Trace core flows: indexing path (input archive -> parse -> storage/index), search path (query parse -> execution -> result formatting).
3) Note data boundaries: DB (SQLite), search index (Tantivy), file I/O paths, and serialization formats.
4) Record a module graph (imports, public APIs, and cross-module calls).

Acceptance:
- A concise but complete architecture summary that identifies the main pipeline stages, storage layers, and execution entry points.
- List of files/functions that are hot paths or correctness-critical.
","notes":"Architecture map (high level):
- Entry point: src/main.rs wires CLI (Cli/Commands) to command handlers: index/search/stats/tweet/list/export/config/update/completions.
- CLI definitions: src/cli.rs declares subcommands + many flags (types/limit/offset/sort/since/until/replies/context/fields, etc.). Some flags are not yet wired in main.rs (noted for later correctness checks).
- Core modules (src/lib.rs): parser, model, storage (SQLite), search (Tantivy), config/logging/perf/error.

Indexing flow:
1) xf index <archive> (main.rs cmd_index): validate archive/data/, resolve db/index paths, optionally clear existing data.
2) ArchiveParser (parser.rs) parses JS-wrapped JSON (window.YTD.*) with rayon; parse_manifest -> ArchiveInfo; parse_tweets/likes/dms/grok/followers/etc into model types.
3) Storage::open (storage.rs) opens SQLite, sets pragmas, migrates schema, and stores data into normalized tables + FTS5 virtual tables.
4) SearchEngine::open (search.rs) opens/creates Tantivy index; writer adds documents for each data type (id/text/text_prefix/type/created_at/metadata) and commits.

Search flow:
1) xf search <query> (main.rs cmd_search): open SearchEngine and Storage; map DataType -> search::DocType; call SearchEngine::search with limit+offset.
2) SearchEngine::search (search.rs) uses Tantivy QueryParser (text + prefix field) and optional type filter; collects TopDocs; builds SearchResult with highlights (snippet generator) and metadata (stored JSON).
3) main.rs formats results to json/json pretty/csv/compact/text (with highlight -> ANSI conversion in print_result).

Data boundaries:
- Input: archive files under <archive>/data/*.js with JS prefix; parser tolerates whitespace and trailing semicolons.
- Storage: SQLite schema for tweets/likes/dms/grok + FTS5 tables; metadata in archive_info/meta tables.
- Search index: Tantivy index under XF_INDEX; schema fields include id, text, text_prefix (prefix matching), type, created_at, metadata.

Hot paths / correctness-critical:
- parser.rs: parse_js_file, parse_* per datatype; date parsing; numeric parsing; message aggregation.
- search.rs: schema construction, index_* methods, query parsing, snippet generation.
- storage.rs: schema/migrations + store_* insertions and FTS5 updates.
